mission.title: open-webui: feat: Create Collections of Document Files
mission.description: feat: Create Collections of Document Files. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
I would like the ability to select multiple documents without having to select each file individually.

I would like a feature that allows for the documents to be grouped by user defined collections.

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: Create Collections of Document Files
steps.description:
For this a citation feature (Like in AnythingLLM) would be helpful as you are looking for collections of docs. 

You could show the name of the doc and the chunks of text received from the chromadb after sending your question in the ui as a citation.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: Create Collections of Document Files" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
For this a citation feature (Like in AnythingLLM) would be helpful as you are looking for collections of docs. 

You could show the name of the doc and the chunks of text received from the chromadb after sending your question in the ui as a citation.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
